stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 11 people in Gaza City, medics say
stories.summary: Women and children were among those killed in the attacks on four residential buildings, which Israel's military says killed four Hamas security officials.

stories.headline: Hezbollah official says group told Lebanon it rejects proposed truce with Israel
stories.summary: A top Iranian general reiterated Tehran's demand for a full ceasefire in Lebanon on Thursday and called for Israel to pull troops back to where they were when the wider war began.

stories.headline: Russia kills 12 in Ukraine as Kyiv mourns 707 children killed since 2022
stories.summary: Russia's latest attacks come as Ukraine commemorates the children killed by Russia over the course of the all-out war.

stories.headline: Massie, 3 Other House Republicans Broke From Trump on Iran War Powers Vote
stories.summary: Four Republicans from different ideological factions crossed party lines to vote with Democrats in favor of reining in the president’s power to wage war unilaterally.

stories.headline: ‘Unpatriotic’: Trump decries Republicans who voted to constrain Iran war
stories.summary: Trump takes aim at lawmakers after House passes war powers resolution; advocates call on Senate to advance the measure.

stories.headline: Hezbollah rejects renewed ceasefire agreed by Israel and Lebanon
stories.summary: The United States announced the ceasefire agreement on Wednesday night following a fresh round of talks.

stories.headline: Trump hits back at 'unpatriotic' vote after House rebukes him over Iran
stories.summary: The lower chamber of Congress passed a measure that seeks to halt further military action, in a vote seen as largely symbolic.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon agree to implement ceasefire if Hezbollah stops attacks
stories.summary: The countries reject "any attempt, by any state or non-state actor, to hold Lebanon's future hostage", the US says.

stories.headline: US-Iran Talks Progress Stalls After Hezbollah Rejects Truce
stories.summary: There was no sign of progress in ceasefire talks between the US and Iran after the worst burst of violence in weeks and as the Tehran-backed Hezbollah militia rejected on Thursday a US-brokered truce in Lebanon.

stories.headline: US Strategic Petroleum Reserve Nears a 3-Year Low
stories.summary: The US buffer for crude oil is shrinking fast as the Iran war drags on. The country’s emergency petroleum stockpile, known as the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, has shrunk to levels last seen in mid-2023, after the Biden administration decided to withdraw SPR crude barrels to mitigate the oil-price impact of the Russia-Ukraine war. The global oil supply chain is becoming more vulnerable with each passing day the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.  El

stories.headline: The four Republicans who broke with Trump on Iran and now face his wrath
stories.summary: There are still hurdles before Congress could force the president to end hostilities, but the House resolution reflects lawmakers' growing impatience, including within Trump's own party.

stories.headline: Global steel crisis deepens as oversupply reaches alarming levels, OECD warns
stories.summary: The global steel industry is facing a growing crisis as subsidised production and excess capacity continue to distort markets, according to the OECD. It says state-backed steel production, particularly in China, is increasing pressure on producers in Europe and other OECD economies.

stories.headline: Kushner-backed luxury resort plan sparks protests in Albania
stories.summary: Demonstrators say the project backed by Donald Trump's son-in-law would harm a protected environment.

stories.headline: Missing Sherpa guide found on Mount Everest after 'miracle' self-rescue
stories.summary: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.

stories.headline: Bondi Beach shooting hero charged with assault
stories.summary: Ahmed al Ahmed disarmed one of the alleged shooters during the deadly attack at a Jewish event.

stories.headline: Trump to nominate Blanche for attorney general on permanent basis
stories.summary: If confirmed by the Senate, Trump's former personal lawyer would become the administration's number one prosecutor.

stories.headline: Germany blames Russia for 'bitter defeat' in UN Security Council bid
stories.summary: Johann Wadephul said it was "no secret" Russia had stirred up sentiment against Germany over its support for Ukraine.

stories.headline: China bans four New Zealand MPs over Taiwan visit
stories.summary: Upon their return from Taiwan last month, the lawmakers were told that China had banned them for a year.

stories.headline: Fifa bans all water bottles from World Cup games
stories.summary: Fifa has been warned it is putting fans' health at risk by introducing a bottle ban at this summer's World Cup.

stories.headline: Miracle on Everest: Guide believed dead spotted crawling down ice
stories.summary: Cleaners found Dawa Sherpa crawling towards Base Camp six days after he went missing at a higher altitude.

stories.headline: Trump family’s planned luxury resort sparks protests in Albania
stories.summary: Demonstrators oppose $4bn resort backed by investment firm founded by US president’s son-in-law Jared Kushner

stories.headline: Saylor’s Bitcoin Machine Is Misfiring on Every Cylinder
stories.summary: Michael Saylor built Strategy Inc. around a straightforward idea: raise money to buy Bitcoin, then never sell it. Over time, that simple bet has turned far more complicated.

stories.headline: Princeton Endowment Backs Out of Oil and Gas Divestment Vow
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Janet Lorin joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Princeton University’s endowment is backtracking on its pledge to divest from publicly traded oil and gas companies, four years after it said it would exit such holdings as way to move toward a net-zero portfolio. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Foreign Buyers Snap Up Turkish Stocks in Fast-Track Deals
stories.summary: Foreign investors are piling into fast-tracked sales of shares in Turkish companies, seeing pockets of value in a market facing political turmoil at home and fallout from the Iran war.

stories.headline: Muni Tobacco Bonds Have First-Ever Default as Smoking Declines
stories.summary: The municipal bond market’s $80 billion tobacco bond sector had its first-ever default after a Nassau County, New York, agency failed to make a $36 million principal payment on June 1.

stories.headline: Diane Carlson Evans Dies at 79; Led Campaign for Vietnam Women’s Memorial
stories.summary: A former Army nurse who served in combat, she coordinated a 10-year effort to create a monument recognizing the contributions of the war’s thousands of nurses.

stories.headline: Jan. 6 Rioter Is Hired to Work in Sensitive Pentagon Office
stories.summary: It was not clear who hired Elias Irizarry, who pleaded guilty to various offenses at the Capitol and was later pardoned by President Trump.

stories.headline: Trump Says He Plans to Nominate Blanche for Attorney General
stories.summary: Todd Blanche has been serving as the acting attorney general. A formal nomination to the post would be the president’s latest move to place loyalists in top government jobs.

stories.headline: Bondi attack hero Ahmed al-Ahmed charged with assaulting his father
stories.summary: Police allege the 44-year-old lauded for disarming one of the gunmen in December’s attack put his father in a headlockGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe Sydney man who disarmed one of the Bondi terror attackers has been charged with assaulting his father.Ahmed al-Ahmed, 44, is accused of allegedly putting his father in a headlock on 9 March at a house in Bankstown, in the city’s west. stories.headline: Disney racks up $4.2bn deficit on Paris parks
stories.summary: Exclusive: Analysis shows resort has yet to recoup Disney’s investment despite record revenue and 16m annual visitorsDisney has still not recouped $4.2bn of its investment in Disneyland Paris after more than 30 years, even though the resort is now its best-performing international outpost, according to an analysis of recent filings.The sprawling theme park complex swung open its ornate iron gates in 1992 and now attracts about 16 million visitors

stories.headline: UK to challenge EU over ‘devastating’ plans to almost halve tariff-free steel import quotas
stories.summary: Business secretary to meet European counterpart on Friday as EU industry leaders worry about retaliatory measures by UKBusiness live – latest updatesThe UK business secretary, Peter Kyle, is to raise concerns about EU plans to dramatically reduce tariff-free imports of British steel with its trade commissioner, Maroš Šefčovič, in Brussels on Friday.The UK steel industry has previously warned of “devastating” consequences from the new quota system

stories.headline: Clash at protest against Kushner linked Albania resort project
stories.summary: Video shows security personnel arguing with demonstrators before pushing one protester to the ground and dragging him away. The incident took place during a rally organised by environmental groups opposing a luxury resort project linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of US President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Trump says Eiffel Tower-like UFC structure may stay
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says he may choose to leave a purpose-built UFC arena standing at the White House because it is "attractive to a lot of people".

stories.headline: Heavy gunfire in Somali capital as row over election delay escalates

stories.headline: Man City consider legal action after Haaland claim

stories.headline: Private Credit Deterioration Slow, Contained: Robson
stories.summary: Meghan Robson, head of US credit strategy at BNP Paribas, and Sonali Pier, portfolio manager: multi-sector credit at PIMCO, join Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Cliffwater, Blackstone, and Partners Group all capped certain fund redemptions this week. The moves put private markets back into the spotlight. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Nasdaq 100 Declines, Dow Jones Hits Record as AI Trade Falters
stories.summary: Investors ditched technology stocks in favor of the so-called “old economy” on Thursday as Broadcom Inc.’s earnings report pumped the brakes on the artificial-intelligence rally.

stories.headline: Education Department Pushes Faster College Mergers
stories.summary: The US Education Department says hundreds of colleges face financial distress and many won't survive the decade. Under Secretary Nicholas Kent join Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why the administration wants to speed up college mergers, defend private capital's role in higher education, and use AI-driven efficiencies to help lower costs for students and families. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Illinois Passes $56B Budget as Bears Deal Stalled
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Shruti Singh joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Illinois legislators early Monday morning passed a nearly $56 billion budget for the fiscal year starting July 1, aiming to ease higher health-care and housing costs while bracing for potential federal funding cuts. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Senators Cassidy and Booker Ask Judge to Maintain Block on $1.8 Billion Fund
stories.summary: Bill Cassidy, a Republican, and Cory Booker, a Democrat, maintained that the fund was “an immediate and dire threat to our constitutional order and the authority of Congress.”

stories.headline: Republicans Defeat Move to Bar Trump From Creating Compensation Fund
stories.summary: Senator Chuck Schumer, Democrat of New York, introduced the motion, which would have sent the G.O.P. immigration bill back to committee. Three Republicans up for re-election voted for it, but it still fell short.

stories.headline: Jamaican police officer charged with murder after woman shot during protest

stories.summary: Wall Street drove a rotation out of chipmakers into several other industries that tend to benefit from a resilient economy after Broadcom Inc.’s underwhelming outlook tested the artificial-intelligence trade.

stories.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

stories.headline: Wars, sanctions and punishments: Iraq's 40-year World Cup wait

stories.summary: Russia is intensifying attacks in Ukraine but more than four years of war are causing concern even among Putin loyalists.

stories.summary: Four people have been arrested and charged with trafficking more than $45 million in cocaine, according to federal officials.

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down

stories.headline: Colombia presidential runoff pits leftist senator against pro-Trump rival

stories.headline: How Putin became master of the image
stories.summary: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

stories.headline: Netanyahu says he has directed IDF to increase control of Gaza to 70%
stories.summary: The expansion in control by Israel would contradict the terms of the ceasefire  Israel and Hamas agreed to in October 2025.

stories.summary: US officials earlier told the BBC that the framework of a ceasefire extension deal had been agreed, pending the approval of Trump and Iran's leadership.

stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.
